channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A 'heat dome' is driving dangerous heat across the U.S. into the July 4 weekend
item.description: Dangerous heat is expected across large swaths of the U.S. this week, according to the National Weather Service.
item.content:encoded:
Dangerous heat is expected across large swaths of the U.S. this week, according to the National Weather Service.

(Image credit: Adam Gray)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Can one bill make housing more affordable?
item.description: Congress passed the most comprehensive housing bill in decades. Housing expert Vincent Reina of the University of Pennsylvania talks about the effort to make housing more affordable.
item.content:encoded: Congress passed the most comprehensive housing bill in decades. Housing expert Vincent Reina of the University of Pennsylvania talks about the effort to make housing more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: The World Cup spotlights Indigenous cultures
item.description: As the World Cup comes to the Pacific Northwest, Indigenous people are welcoming fans with ceremonies, music and culture while sharing the history of the land. Lauren Gallup reports from Seattle.
item.content:encoded: As the World Cup comes to the Pacific Northwest, Indigenous people are welcoming fans with ceremonies, music and culture while sharing the history of the land. Lauren Gallup reports from Seattle.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallup

item.title: Beyond the Shore podcast uncovers the hidden world of the Great Lakes
item.description: In Michigan Public's new podcast Beyond the Shore, host Rebecca Williams uncovers the shipwrecks, remarkable wildlife and hidden ecosystems beneath America's Great Lakes.
item.content:encoded: In Michigan Public's new podcast Beyond the Shore, host Rebecca Williams uncovers the shipwrecks, remarkable wildlife and hidden ecosystems beneath America's Great Lakes.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: A bridge to Canada may be blocked by the Trump administration
item.description: The Gordie Howe bridge spans the most important border crossing between the U.S. and Canada. President Donald Trump has said he doesn't want it open yet.
item.content:encoded:
The Gordie Howe bridge spans the most important border crossing between the U.S. and Canada. President Donald Trump has said he doesn't want it open yet.

(Image credit: Henry Larson)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: 3 firefighters killed on Colorado-Utah border as wildfires intensify
item.description: The deaths occurred as crews battled multiple fires across a parched region. Two other firefighters were injured.
item.content:encoded:
The deaths occurred as crews battled multiple fires across a parched region. Two other firefighters were injured.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Not just New York: How New Mexico's free childcare policy is doing
item.description: Reporter Katerina Barton tells NPR's Ayesha Rascoe about New Mexico's plan to provide free childcare for state residents.
item.content:encoded: Reporter Katerina Barton tells NPR's Ayesha Rascoe about New Mexico's plan to provide free childcare for state residents.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Juneberries are still very much in season. These farmers want you to try them
item.description: Juneberries are very much in season. Also called serviceberries, they are not widely available in the United States. Now there's a new push to get more growing on farms.
item.content:encoded: Juneberries are very much in season. Also called serviceberries, they are not widely available in the United States. Now there's a new push to get more growing on farms.
item.dc:creator: Kate Grumke

item.title: 5 years after the Surfside condo collapse, a surviving family struggles to move on
item.description: The Gonzalez family is struggling to rebuild and recover from debilitating injuries five years after the deadly collapse of a beachfront building in the Miami Beach town of Surfside, Fla.
item.content:encoded: The Gonzalez family is struggling to rebuild and recover from debilitating injuries five years after the deadly collapse of a beachfront building in the Miami Beach town of Surfside, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: Win the family cookout with perfectly cooked hot dogs
item.description: Microwave or air fryer? Grill or slow cooker? An investigation into how to cook hot dogs for the most flavor and the most joy.
item.content:encoded:
Microwave or air fryer? Grill or slow cooker? An investigation into how to cook hot dogs for the most flavor and the most joy.

(Image credit: Ayesha Rascoe)
item.dc:creator: Dianna Douglas
